channel.title: Chronic Mystery
channel.description: When You're Exhausted and Nobody Can Tell You Why

item.title: Brain Fog Is Not in Your Head
item.description: The neuroinflammation behind cognitive dysfunction in ME/CFS and why dismissing it as stress is medically irresponsible

item.title: The Energy Envelope Explained
item.description: The single most important concept in ME/CFS management — and why staying inside your envelope is harder than it sounds

item.title: The Research They Don't Fund
item.description: Why ME/CFS receives a fraction of the research funding of comparable conditions — and what that says about medical priorities

item.title: Imposter Syndrome in Chronic Illness
item.description: Feeling like you're not sick enough to claim the identity — even on the days you can't get out of bed

item.title: The Genetic Vulnerability
item.description: What twin studies and genetic research tell us about who develops chronic fatigue — and who doesn't

item.title: Coagulation Abnormalities in Fatigue
item.description: Microclotting, fibrin deposition, and the blood flow problems that contribute to fatigue and brain fog

item.title: The Rest Schedule
item.description: Proactive rest, scheduled downtime, and why waiting until you're exhausted to rest is already too late

item.title: The Identity of Rest
item.description: Reclaiming rest as an identity rather than a failure — the radical act of being still in a world that won't stop moving

item.title: Managing Multiple Conditions
item.description: When ME/CFS comes with POTS, fibromyalgia, EDS, and MCAS — coordinating management across overlapping conditions

item.title: The Grief Ritual
item.description: Creating space to mourn what chronic illness has taken — because unexpressed grief becomes another weight your body carries

item.title: Autonomic Testing Guide
item.description: Tilt table, QSART, heart rate variability — the tests that map your autonomic dysfunction and what the results mean

item.title: Reclaiming the Body
item.description: Moving from adversarial to collaborative — the practice of befriending a body that seems to have become your opponent

item.title: The Social Media Energy Drain
item.description: How scrolling costs more energy than you think — and strategies for staying connected without crashing

item.title: Hormonal Chaos in Chronic Fatigue
item.description: Thyroid, cortisol, and sex hormone disruptions that fuel fatigue — and why standard ranges miss the dysfunction

item.title: Rest as Resistance
item.description: In a culture that profits from your exhaustion, choosing to rest is a radical act — especially when your body leaves you no choice

item.title: The Gut-Fatigue Connection
item.description: How microbiome disruption drives systemic inflammation and fatigue in ways most gastroenterologists still don't test for

item.title: Contemplative Reading Practice
item.description: Lectio divina and slow reading — how to engage with text when your brain can only handle a paragraph at a time

item.title: Yoga Nidra for Chronic Fatigue
item.description: The practice of conscious rest — why lying down and being guided into deep relaxation might be the most therapeutic thing you can do

item.title: Chronic Illness and Faith
item.description: How illness tests, transforms, or destroys spiritual beliefs — and what emerges from the wreckage

item.title: Craniosacral Therapy Evidence
item.description: The gentle bodywork that some ME/CFS patients swear by — what the research says and what the mechanism might be
